headline: Heather Cove Villas: 28-unit 55+ development approved for south Toledo with 15-year tax abatement
paragraphs: Eagle Creek Builders is developing Heather Cove Villas, a 28-unit 55-and-older community on a 4-acre lot at 3322 Schneider Road in south Toledo's Glendale-Heatherdowns neighborhood. The Toledo City Plan Commission approved the zoning change and site plan; the city is supporting the project with a 15-year property-tax abatement. Pre-construction prices start at $229,900 for two-bedroom, two-bath villa-style homes with attached garages, covered porches, and sunrooms. The Blade reported on the project May 28.

The Schneider Road site sits behind an AutoZone, close to Heatherdowns-area shopping, the University of Toledo Medical Center, and Heather-Stebner Park. A private street and on-site parking are included in the plan. Eagle Creek is a Toledo-based homebuilder with an established track record in the local market.
why_it_matters: 55-and-older housing is one of the most consistently undersupplied segments of Toledo's residential market — and it also frees up existing single-family inventory when downsizers make the move. A 28-unit market-rate development at $229,900 in south Toledo tests whether new construction can pencil without deep subsidy outside the Vistula-Warehouse District core. The 15-year tax abatement signals that the city's residential incentive toolkit is reaching into legacy neighborhoods, not just the downtown investment zone. If pre-sales track well, Heather Cove validates a replicable model for other aging commercial or vacant parcels along south Toledo's Secor-Heatherdowns-Sylvania corridor.
